questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A square gate of size $4 m \times 4 m$ is hinged at topmost point. A liquid of density $\rho$ fills the space left of it. The force which acting at a height 1 m from lowest point can hold the gate stationary is $\frac{2^N}{0} \rho g$. The value of ' N ' is
questions.solution:
Force acting on any elementary strip at a distance $y$ from O
$d F=(\rho g y)(a d y)$

Torque about O
$\begin{aligned} d \tau & =y(d F) \\ d \tau & =y(d F) \\ d \tau & =\left(\rho g y^2 a\right) d y\end{aligned}$

Net torque $\tau=\int_0^a\left(\rho g y^2\right) d y=\left.\rho g a \frac{y^2}{3}\right|_0 ^a$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A conical vessel is completely filled with a liquid of density $\sigma=\frac{3}{4 \pi R^3}$ as shown in the figure. The force exerted by the liquid on the curved surface is ____ N.
questions.solution:
as $\mathrm{m} \vec{g}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}_{\text {bybowam }}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}_{\text {carred surface }}=0$

$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \pi R^2 R \cdot \frac{3}{4 \pi R^3} g \cdot \bar{j}-\frac{3}{4 \pi R^3} g R \pi R^2 \bar{j}^{-} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{g}}{4} \mathrm{j}-\frac{3 \mathrm{~g}}{4} \overline{\mathrm{j}}=-\frac{\mathrm{g}}{2} \mathrm{j}=-5 \mathrm{jN} \\ & F=5 \mathrm{~N}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Water is filled in a uniform container of area of cross section A. A hole of cross section area a ( $\lt \lt A)$ is made in the container at a height of 20 m above the base. Water streams out and hits a small block placed at some distance from container. With what speed (in $\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$ ) the block should be moved such that water streams always hits the block. (Given $\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{A}}=\frac{1}{20}$ ). (Take $\mathrm{g}=10$ $\mathrm{ms}^{-2}$ )
questions.solution: Velocity of efflux $v=\sqrt{2 g y}$ Range $x=\sqrt{2 g y} \times \sqrt{\frac{2 h}{g}}$ The velocity of the block must be $\left(\frac{d x}{d t}\right)$. $\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad V_b & =\frac{d x}{d t}=\sqrt{\frac{2 h}{g}} \times \sqrt{2 g} \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{y}} \frac{d y}{d t} \\ V_b & =\frac{\sqrt{h}}{\sqrt{y}} \cdot \frac{d y}{d t} \end{aligned}$ ..(i) Using equation of continuity $\frac{\text { Ady }}{d t}=a \sqrt{2 g y}$ ..(ii) $\begin{aligned} & \text { equation (i) and (ii) } \\ & \qquad \begin{array}{l}V_b=\sqrt{\frac{h}{y}} \times \frac{a}{A} \sqrt{2 g y} \\ V_b=\sqrt{2 g h} \times \frac{a}{A} \\ =20 \times \frac{1}{20}=1 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}\end{array}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A long glass capillary tube of radius r is placed horizontally and filled with water (angle of contact for water glass $=0^{\circ}$ ). If the tube is made vertical, then the length of water column that remains in the capillary is $\frac{\eta T}{r \rho g}$ where ' T ' is the surface tension of water, $\rho$ is density of water. Find $\eta$
questions.solution: $P_0-\frac{2 T}{R}+\rho g h-P_0=\frac{2 T}{R} \Rightarrow h=\frac{4 T}{R \rho g}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A cubical block of volume $v$ and density $3 \rho$ is placed inside a liquid of density $\rho$ and attached to a spring of spring constant k as shown in the figure. Assuming ideal spring and pulley and spring is attached at $A$ which is at $\frac{R}{2}$ from centre. The compression in the spring at equilibrium is $\frac{x p v g}{k}$, where $\mathrm{x}=$
questions.solution:
Tension in the string $T=m g-F_B=3 \rho v g-\rho v g=2 \rho v g$
Balancing torque $k x R \backslash 2=T R$ $\Rightarrow x=2 T / k=4 \rho V g / k$ An; $\mathrm{x}=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Oil enters the bend of a pipe in the horizontal plane with velocity $4 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ and pressure $280 \times 10^3 \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$ as shown in the figure. The pressure of oil at the point Q is $86 \lambda$. Find the value of $\lambda .\left(\mathrm{KNm}^{-2}\right)$. (Take specific gravity of oil as 0.9 and $\sin 37^{\circ}=0.6$ )
questions.solution:
Let $A_1$ and $A_2$ be the cross-sectional area of the pipe at points $P$ and $Q$ respectively. Let $\mathrm{v}_1$ and $\mathrm{v}_2$ be the velocities of oil at the points P and Q respectively. By conservation of mass,
$\mathrm{Q}=\mathrm{A}_1 \mathrm{~V}_1=\mathrm{A}_2 \mathrm{~V}_2$
$\Rightarrow \quad v_2=\left(\frac{A_1}{A_2}\right)\left(v_1\right)=4 v_1$
Applying Bernoullis's equation between points $P$ and $Q$, we have
$P_1+\frac{1}{2} \rho v_1^2=P_2+\frac{1}{2} \rho v_2^2$
$P_2=P_1+\frac{1}{2} p\left(v_1^2-v_2^2\right)$
$=P_1+\frac{1}{2} \times 0.9[16-256] \times 10^3$
$=280 \times 10^3+(-108) \times 10^3$
$P_2=172 \times 10^3 \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$
$P_2=172\left(\mathrm{KN} \mathrm{m}^{-2}\right)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The tap in the garden was closed in appropriately resulting in the water flowing freely out of it which forms a downward narrowing beam. The beam of water has a circular cross-section, the diameter of the circle is 6 mm at one point and 3 cm below it is only 3 mm as shown in figure. If the rate of water wasted is $(x \times \pi) \mathrm{mL} /$ minute then find the value of $x$. (Neglect the effect of viscosity and surface tension of the flowing water.)
questions.solution:
From continuity equation; $A_1 v_1=A_2 v_2 \Rightarrow 4 v_1=v_2 \ldots$ (i)
From Bernoulli's sequation $\frac{\rho v_1^2}{2}=\frac{\rho v_2^2}{2}-\rho g h$..
Solving (i) \& (ii); $v_1=\frac{1}{5} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} ; v_2=\frac{4}{5} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
Total volume wasted $=\pi\left(\frac{6 \times 10^{-3}}{2}\right)^2 v_1 \times t=\pi \times 9 \times 10^{-6} \times \frac{1}{5} \times 60 \times 1000=108 \pi \mathrm{~mL} / \mathrm{minute}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A vessel containing liquid of density $\rho$ is accelerated horizontally with acceleration $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ in a gravity free space. A small sphere of density $2 \rho$ is released from rest with respect to the vessel. Find the initial acceleration of the sphere with respect to the vessel:
questions.solution:
$F_{\text {net }}=F_{\text {pseudo }}-F_{\text {Buoyant }}=V \times 2 \rho \times 10-v \rho \times 10=v \rho \times 10$
$a=\frac{F_{\text {net }}}{n}=\frac{v \rho \times 10}{v \times 2 \rho}=5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An open tank having dimensions $1 \mathrm{~m} \times 2 \mathrm{~m} \times 3 \mathrm{~m}$ completely filled with water is kept on a horizontal surface. The mass of water that spills out is $100 x \mathrm{~kg}$, when the tank is slowly accelerated horizontally at the rate of $2 \mathrm{~ms}^2$. Then find the value of $x$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \tan \theta=\mathrm{a}_o / \mathrm{g} \Rightarrow \tan \theta=2 / 10 \Rightarrow \tan \theta=0.2 \\ & \tan \theta=\mathrm{y} / 2 \backslash \mathrm{y}=0.4 \mathrm{~m} \\ & \text { p volume of shaded region } \\ & \mathrm{p} \mathrm{V}=(1 / 2)^{\prime} 0.4^{\prime} 2^{\prime} \mathrm{l}=0.4 \mathrm{~m}^3 \\ & \text { mass }=\mathrm{V}^{\prime} \mathrm{d}=0.4^{\prime} 1000=400 \mathrm{~kg} \\ & \mathrm{p} \mathrm{x}-4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Water is filled in a uniform container of area of cross section $A$. $A$ hole of cross section area $a(\lt \lt A)$ is made to the wall of container at a height of 20 m above the base. Water streams out and hits a small block placed at some distance from container. With what speed (in $\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$ ) should the block be moved such that water stream always hits the block before the level of water in the vessel reaches $20 \mathrm{~m} .\left(\right.$ Given $\left.\frac{a}{A}=\frac{1}{20}\right)$ (Take $g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$ )
questions.solution: Velocity of efflux $v=\sqrt{2 g y}$ Range $x=\sqrt{2 g y} \times \sqrt{\frac{2 h}{g}}$ The velocity of the block must be $\left(\frac{d x}{d t}\right)$. $\therefore \quad V_b=\frac{d x}{d t}=\sqrt{\frac{2 h}{g}} \times \sqrt{2 g} \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{y}} \frac{d y}{d t}$ $V_b=\frac{\sqrt{h}}{\sqrt{y}} \cdot \frac{d y}{d t} \ldots \ldots .(\mathrm{i})$ Using equation of continuity $\frac{A d y}{d t}=a \sqrt{2 g y}.......(ii)$ equation (i) and (ii) $V_b=\sqrt{\frac{h}{y}} \times \frac{a}{A} \sqrt{2 g y}$ $V_b=\sqrt{2 g h} \times \frac{a}{A}=20 \times \frac{1}{20}=1 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a capillary tube of length 20 cm and bore diameter 1 mm , a very thin glass rod of diameter 0.5 mm is coaxially arranged. The arrangement is dipped in water for which angle of contact with glass is $0^{\circ}$. What is the difference in length of capillary raise if the tube is vertical in a case and is at $30^{\circ}$ to the horizontal in other? Express the magnitude in cm . Surface tension of water is $0.075 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$
questions.solution:
For capillary raise,
$\mathrm{h} \rho \mathrm{g}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~T} \cos }{(\mathrm{R}-\mathrm{r})} \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}=6 \mathrm{~cm}$
and at $30^{\circ}, \ell=\frac{\mathrm{h}}{\sin \theta}=2 \mathrm{~h}$
$\therefore$ difference, $\Delta \ell=6 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A liquid is kept in a cylindrical vessel. When the vessel is rotated about its axis, the liquid rises at its sides. If the radius of the vessel is 0.05 m and the speed of rotation is 2 revolutions per second, the difference in the heights (in cm ) of the liquid at the centre and at the sides of the vessel is (take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$ and $\pi^2=10$ )
questions.solution:
Using Bernoulli's theorem
$\frac{1}{2} \rho v^2=p=\rho g h \quad \text { or } \quad h=\frac{v^2}{2 g h}$    .......(i)
Put: $V=r \omega=r(2 \pi v)$ in (1)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
When a long glass capillary tube of radius 0.015 cm is dipped in a liquid, the liquid rises to a height of 15 cm within it. If the contact angle between the liquid and glass to close to $0^{\circ}$, the surface tension of the liquid, in milli Newton $\mathrm{m}^{-1}$, is $\qquad$ $\left[\rho_{(\text {liquid })}=900 \mathrm{kgm}^{-3}, \mathrm{~g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right]$
(Give answer in closest integer)
questions.solution:
Given : Radius of capillary tube, $\mathrm{r}=0.015 \mathrm{~cm}=15 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~mm}$
$\mathrm{h}=15 \mathrm{~cm}=15 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~mm}$

Using, $\mathrm{h}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~T} \cos \theta}{\rho \mathrm{gr}} \quad\left[\cos \theta=\cos 0^{\circ}=1\right]$
Surface tension, $T=\frac{\mathrm{rhpg}}{2}=\frac{15 \times 10^{-5} \times 15 \times 10^{-2} \times 900 \times 10}{2}=101$ milli newton $\mathrm{m}^{-1}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An open cylindrical vessel filled with water is rotated together with water at angular speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>rad</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. At this angular speed, the centre of the bottom is just exposed. If the angular speed is increased to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi>rad</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, how much area (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>cm</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>) of the bottom of the vessel is now exposed to surrounding? [Area of the cross-section of cylinder is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cm</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>]
questions.solution:
Let ω1 be initial angular velocity

ω12R22g=h
For angular velocity ω2 let radius of exposed surface be x

ω222gR2-x2=h
Putting the value of ω1 and ω2
πx2=9
Since πR2=27
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two objects of equal volume $V=1 \mathrm{~m}^3$ and different densities $d_1=500 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$ and $\mathrm{d}_2=1000 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$ are glued to each other so that their contact surface is flat and has an area $\mathrm{A}=0.1 \mathrm{~m}^2$. When the objects are submerged completely in a certain liquid, they float in stable equilibrium, the contact surface being parallel to the surface of the liquid (see the diagram).
questions.solution: We have $\rho_{\ell} 2 g=1500 \mathrm{~g} \Rightarrow \rho_{\ell}=750 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}^3$ The area joined by glue is not exposed to the liquid. P is the pressure at glued part $=\rho_{\ell} g H$ $\therefore$ For not breaking $\rho_{\ell} V g-\rho_{\ell} g H A=F+\rho_1 g V \Rightarrow H=3 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows a cubical block of side 10 cm and relative density 1.5 suspended by a wire of cross sectional area $10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}^2$. The breaking stress of the wire is $7 \times 10^6 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$. The block is placed in a beaker of base area $200 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and initially i.e. at $\mathrm{t}=0$, the top surface of water \& the block coincide. There is a pump at the bottom corner whichejects $2 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of water per sec. Find the time (in sec) at which the wire will break.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& T_{\max }=7 \times 10^6 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~N}=7 \mathrm{~N} \\
& \therefore F_{B_{\min }}=(15-7)=8 \mathrm{~N}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Water level its be lowered $=2 \mathrm{~cm}$
$\therefore$ Area occupied by water level at the top $=100 \mathrm{~cm}^2$
$\therefore 2 \mathrm{t}=100 \times 2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=100 \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A block of wood is floating in water such that $1 / 3^{\text {rd }}$ of it is submerged in water when the same block is floated in alcohol, $1 / 2$ of it's volume is submerged. Now a mixture of water and alcohol is made taking equal volume of both and block is floated in it. What is the \% of it's volume that is now submerged.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A square plate of 1 m side moves parallel to a second plate with velocity $4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. A thin layer of water exist between plates. If the viscous force is 2 N and the coeff of viscosity is 0.01 poise then the distance between the plates in mmis.
questions.solution: $\mathrm{F}=\frac{\eta \mathrm{Av}}{\mathrm{d}} ; \mathrm{d}=\frac{\eta \mathrm{A} v}{\mathrm{~F}}=\frac{0.01 \times 10^{-1} \times 1^2 \times 4}{0.002}=2 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For the arrangement shown in the figure. The time interval in seconds after which the water jet ceases to cross the wall is found to be $(A)^3$. Area of the cross section of the tank $A=\sqrt{5} \mathrm{~m}^2$ and area of the orifice $a=32 \mathrm{~cm}^2$. [Assume that the container remaining fixed]. Find the value of $\alpha$. (Take $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$)
questions.solution: For minimum velocity at orifice $\Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{v}_{\min }\right) \times \sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{~h}}{\mathrm{~g}}}=1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}_{\min }=\sqrt{5} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ This will give us minimum height for this velocity $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \sqrt{2 g h_{\operatorname{mh}}}=\sqrt{5} \Rightarrow h_{\min }=\frac{1}{4} \mathrm{~m}=0.25 \mathrm{~m} \\ & \mathrm{v}_1=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{gh}} \& \mathrm{v}_2=-\mathrm{dh} / \mathrm{dt} \end{aligned}$ By equation of continuity $\mathrm{av}_1=\mathrm{Av}_2$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{a} \sqrt{2 \mathrm{gh}}=\mathrm{A}\left(-\frac{\mathrm{dh}}{\mathrm{dt}}\right) \Rightarrow \int_{0.81}^{0.25} \frac{\mathrm{dh}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{~h}}}=\int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \frac{(\mathrm{a} \sqrt{2 \mathrm{~g}})}{\mathrm{A}} \mathrm{dt} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=125 \mathrm{sec}=(5)^3=(\mathrm{A})^3 \Rightarrow \alpha=5 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A glass plate of area $A$ and mass $m$ is hinged along one of its sides. At what speed the air should be blown parallel to its upper surface to hold the plate horizontal ? The density of air is $\rho$. Give the answer in term of $\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{18 \rho \mathrm{~A}}}$
questions.solution:
Applying Bernoulli's equation up and down the plate $\frac{1}{2} \rho v^2=\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{A}}$
$$
v=\sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{mg}}{\rho \mathrm{~A}}}=\sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{mg} \times 18}{\rho \mathrm{~A} \times 18}}=6\left(\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{18 \rho \mathrm{~A}}}\right)=x \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{18 \rho \mathrm{~A}}} \Rightarrow x=6
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
